creator: Guster
description: Guster The Pasty Tapes 3 released December 15, 2005 Extracted from an original EP and compressed to FLAC, all with xACT 1.62. 01. Manifest Destiny/Sorority Tears [04:11.02] 02. Days [02:55.20] 03. The Sun Shines Down On Me [03:06.56] 04. Dear Valentine [04:41.55] 05. On My Own [05:38.37] Total: [20:33.20] Notes: The Pasty Tapes are EP's sent exclusively to Guster's sales representatives. If you want a copy, talk to a rep; Guster promotes burning copies of The Pasty Tapes for non-reps. You should never pay for a Rep EP. Liner notes: Guster The Pasty Tapes 3 The third installment of the Pasty Tapes finds our fair-skinned friend espoused, off the road, and deep in the throes of domestic bliss. Long story short... Michael "Pasty" Corcoran became a Guster Rep ten years ago at the age of 18. By 19 he'd usurped the bootleg industry at guster.com, by 21 he'd booked us to play at Villanova University four times, and by 23 he was captain of the Gusbus, tour managing our operation with an iron fist and occasional mercy. After gracing two album covers and pushing the crazy strobe light button one too many times, Pasty checked out of the touring world last year at age 28, but he's still the namesake of our special thanks-for-being-a-rep disc, and he's still subject to Guster-photoshopped ridicule, even at the seemingly-safe haven of his own wedding. The five and a half songs on this disc are a collection of forthcoming tracks, rare covers, and album rejects that we hope you'll enjoy and distribute far and wide. No selling the Pasty Tapes. The goal is to get the word out about or new album, "GANGING UP ON THE SUN," which will be out in April. As always, thanks for your help spreading our music. Very very gratefully, Brian, Ryan, Adam, and Joe 1. Manifest Destiny/Sorority Tears (rough mix) From the forthcoming album, "GANGING UP ON THE SUN,", though it's most likely to appear on the new record without the cross-faded back half. I mean, we're very proud of our first instrumental track, but we're not sure we'd want to listen to it every time through our album. Perfect for the Pasty Tapes. 2. Days This is one of our favorite songs by one of our favorite bands, The Kinks. Someone in England asked us to cover the song for a movie they were making and we couldn't say no. Whether that movie will ever come out remains to be seen. They say Sally Field is supposed to be in it. 3. The Sun Shines Down On Me This appeared on a tribute compilation called "The Late Great Daniel Johnston: Discovered, Covered." Daniel Johnston isn't dead, but he is a little obese and kind of schizophrenic. And a genius songwriter to boot. We released this song on the limited edition UK E.P. "Come Downstairs and Say Hello." 4. Dear Valentine (live) Recorded live in Bosotn, with a little help from our friends. The studio version of this song will be on the new album. 5. On My Own There wasn't really room for this one on the album, but that's why we're glad to have the Pasty Tapes. That's Joe singing. Joe's just a tree.
title: Guster: The Pasty Tapes, vol. 3
